name: Buttermilk Doughnuts I
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat fat fryer to 350°F.
2. Mix the eggs, sugar, oil, and buttermilk with a wire whisk until the sugar is dissolved and the yolks are broken up and distributed.
3. Add the spices, vanilla, baking powder, baking soda, and salt; mix well.
4. Fold the flour into the mixture until the ingredients are no longer wet.
5. Let sit for 5 to 10 minutes. The batter will thicken by itself without needing more flour, which would toughen the doughnuts.
6. Drop half of the dough onto a floured board and knead lightly into a ball with folded ends underneath.
7. Roll the dough into a round shape about 1/2 inch thick.
8. Cut with a doughnut cutter.
9. Knead the leftover dough into the second half of the mixture.
10. Repeat kneading and rolling, using very little flour. Dust the dough very lightly; it should not be sticky if left to rest for 5 to 10 minutes as instructed.
11. Cook the doughnuts in hot oil (1 inch deep) in a pan until browned for about 1 1/2 minutes on each side.
12. Makes 30 doughnuts and 30 holes.
